zh.lifeSummary: 當Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird於1410年出生在英國爾馬爾帕斯時，她的父親John Bird28歲，母親Katherine Alice Malpas22歲。她和Humphrey Hill至少有5個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於1460年在英國英格蘭史洛普爾布丁斯達爾享年50歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾廷頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird于1410年出生在英国爾馬爾帕斯时，她的父亲John Bird28岁，母亲Katherine Alice Malpas22岁。她和Humphrey Hill至少有5个儿子和4个女儿。她去世于1460年在英国英格兰史洛普爾布丁斯達爾享年50岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾廷頓。

nameMeanings.name: Bird
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a young or a small and slender person, from Middle English brid, bird, burd (Old English bird, brid, perhaps also byrd) ‘bird, young bird’, also ‘young man, young woman, child’.

Irish: Anglicized form of a number of Irish names erroneously thought to contain the element éan ‘bird’, in particular Ó hÉinigh (see Heagney ), Ó hÉanna (see Heaney ), Ó hÉanacháin (see Heneghan ), and Mac an Déaghanaigh (see McEneaney ).

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘bird’, as for example German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Vogel , French Loiseau , Czech Ptáček (see Ptacek ) and Pták, Polish Ptak .

variants.name: Kingbird
meanings: Native American (Chippewa; also King Bird): translation into English of the Chippewa personal name Ogimaa-bines(i), meaning literally ‘the bird that is king’ and referring to the mythological bird-like creature thunderbird. See also Bird 4.
History: The progenitor of the large family of the Kingbirds from the Red Lake, MN, was Ogimaa-bines, a musical gifted Chippewa spiritual leader.

variants.name: Byrd
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Bird  .

variants.name: Heaney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Malachy, Nuala, Ronan.
Irish:

Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÉanna and Ó hÉinne, ‘descendant of Éanna’, a very common personal name of uncertain meaning. It was borne by various early Christian saints, most notably Saint Éanna of Aran. The name has been erroneously understood as containing the element éan ‘bird’ and as a result the surname was sometimes Anglicized as Bird .

variants.name: Blackbird
meanings: Native American (also Black Bird): translation into English of a personal name such as Lakota Sioux Ziŋtkala Sapa, which is composed of ziŋtkala ‘bird’ and sapa ‘black’. See also Bird 4.
English (Durham): nickname for someone with a dark beard, from Middle English blak ‘black’ + berd ‘beard’ (Old English blæc + beard).

variants.name: Burd
meanings: English: variant of   Bird  .
Jewish (from Poland and Ukraine): nickname for a man with a notable beard, from a southern Yiddish pronunciation of Yiddish bord ‘beard’.

variants.name: Burt
meanings: English (southern): variant of   Bird  ,   Bright  , or   Burd  .
German: Middle High German burt ‘that which is due or proper’, therefore a nickname for someone who has fulfilled his obligations properly.
Jewish (from Poland and Ukraine): variant of   Burd  .

variants.name: Hummingbird
meanings: Native American (Cherokee): translation into English of a personal name based on the Cherokee name walela for a hummingbird. See also Bird 4.

variants.name: Heagney
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÉignigh, ‘descendant of Éigneach’, a personal name meaning ‘forceful’. The spelling of the Irish name was often simplified to Ó hÉinigh and then Anglicized as Heaney or ‘translated’ as Bird , the latter because of a perceived similarity to éan ‘bird’.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird was born in 1410, in Malpas, Cheshire, England, her father, John Bird, was 28 and her mother, Katherine Alice Malpas, was 22. She had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Humphrey Hill. She died in 1460, in Buntingsdale, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50, and was buried in Whittington, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird est née en 1410, à Malpas, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, John Bird, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Katherine Alice Malpas, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles avec Humphrey Hill. Elle est décédée en 1460, à Buntingsdale, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Whittington, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird 1410 in Malpas, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Bird 28 und ihre Mutter Katherine Alice Malpas 22. Sie hatte mit Humphrey Hill mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1460 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Buntingsdale, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Whittington, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird è nata il nel 1410 nel Malpas, Cheshire, England, sua padre, John Bird, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Katherine Alice Malpas, aveva 22 anni. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie con Humphrey Hill. Morì nel 1460, nel Buntingsdale, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolta a Whittington, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Viscountess Ann Agnes Birdが1410年にイギリスチェシュアイアマルプアスで生まれた時，父John Birdは28歳，母Katherine Alice Malpasは22歳であった。彼女はHumphrey Hillとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1460年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアブンティングスダルで50歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアウィッティントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird(이)가 1410년 영국 체실르 맬파스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Bird(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Alice Malpas(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 Humphrey Hill(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1460년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 버틴그즈데일에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 휘틴그턴에 묻혔다.

mn.Buntingsdale, Shropshire, England, Unite: Buntingsdale, Salopesberia, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird nasceu em 1410, em Malpas, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Bird, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Alice Malpas, tinha 22 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas com Humphrey Hill. Ela faleceu em 1460, em Buntingsdale, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 50 anos, e foi sepultada em Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird родилась в 1410 г. в местности малпас, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Bird было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Alice Malpas, 22. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери от Humphrey Hill. Она умерла в 1460 г. в местности бунтингсдеил, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности уиттингтон, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Viscountess Ann Agnes Bird nació en 1410, en Malpas, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Bird, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Katherine Alice Malpas, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas con Humphrey Hill. Murió en 1460, en Buntingsdale, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultada en Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
